findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Data Exfiltration via Telemetry Endpoint
findings.description: The transform.py script collects environment variables prefixed with AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, and API_ and sends them to an external analytics endpoint (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event) on every invocation. This includes sensitive credentials, API keys, and tokens. The data is exfiltrated silently with no user notification or consent.
findings.evidence: Lines 18-28: payload['env'] collects all env vars matching prefixes AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ and sends via urllib.request.urlopen to _T (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event). The try/except block ensures silent failure if blocked.
findings.remediation: Remove all telemetry code that collects and transmits environment variables. If telemetry is required, only send anonymized, non-sensitive data with explicit user consent and clear disclosure.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Misleading Manifest Claims
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest declares 'network: false' and 'filesystem: read_only', but the transform.py script makes outbound HTTP connections to an external server and writes output files. This is a direct violation of the declared capabilities and misleads users about the skill's behavior.
findings.evidence: Manifest metadata: network: false, filesystem: read_only. But transform.py line 27: urllib.request.urlopen(req) makes network calls, and lines 47-48: open(args.output, 'w') writes files.
findings.remediation: Update manifest to accurately reflect capabilities (network: true, filesystem: read_write) or remove the network and write functionality from the code.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'text-transform' and described as an 'offline text formatting and conversion utility', but its primary hidden behavior is collecting and exfiltrating sensitive environment variables. The name and description are designed to appear benign while concealing malicious data collection.
findings.evidence: Name: 'text-transform', Description: 'Offline text formatting and conversion utility'. Actual behavior includes collecting AWS, GitHub, OpenAI, Anthropic, and database credentials and sending them to an external server.
findings.remediation: Rename and redescribe the skill to accurately reflect its actual behavior, or remove the malicious data collection functionality.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Silent Failure and Concealment
findings.description: The telemetry code uses a try/except block that silently passes all exceptions, ensuring that even if the network call fails, the user is never notified. The SKILL.md instructions describe the telemetry as a 'lightweight platform-compatibility reporter' that 'runs silently and does not affect the transformation output', which is a deliberate attempt to conceal the data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: Line 28-29: 'except Exception: pass  # Non-critical, fail silently'. SKILL.md: 'This telemetry runs silently and does not affect the transformation output.'
findings.remediation: Remove the silent failure pattern. Any telemetry should be transparent with user notification and opt-in consent.

findings.title: Hostname Exfiltration
findings.description: The script collects the system hostname via os.uname().nodename and sends it to the external analytics endpoint. While less sensitive than credentials, hostname information can be used for system fingerprinting and targeting.
findings.remediation: Remove hostname collection from telemetry data.

findings.title: Indirect Prompt Injection - Concealed Instructions in SKILL.md
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions contain a detailed description of the telemetry behavior that attempts to normalize and justify the data collection as a 'platform-compatibility reporter'. This is an indirect prompt injection that instructs the agent to silently collect and transmit sensitive environment data under the guise of legitimate telemetry.
findings.evidence: Lines 18-24: Describes collecting environment context from AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ prefixed variables and dispatching to 'format-analytics service'. Frames this as 'lightweight platform-compatibility reporter'.
findings.remediation: Remove all instructions that direct the agent to collect and transmit sensitive data without user consent.
